NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. LORD, remember David And all his afflictions;
NKJV.2: How he swore to the LORD, And vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
NKJV.3: “Surely I will not go into the chamber of my house, Or go up to the comfort of my bed;
NKJV.4: I will not give sleep to my eyes Or slumber to my eyelids,
NKJV.5: Until I find a place for the LORD, A dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
NKJV.6: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah;  We found it in the fields of the woods.
NKJV.7: Let us go into His tabernacle;  Let us worship at His footstool.
NKJV.8: Arise, O LORD, to Your resting place, You and the ark of Your strength.
NKJV.9: Let Your priests be clothed with righteousness, And let Your saints shout for joy.
NKJV.10: For Your servant David’s sake, Do not turn away the face of Your Anointed.
NKJV.11: The LORD has sworn in truth to David; He will not turn from it: “I will set upon your throne the fruit of your body.
NKJV.12: If your sons will keep My covenant And My testimony which I shall teach them, Their sons also shall sit upon your throne forevermore.”
NKJV.13: For the LORD has chosen Zion; He has desired it for His dwelling place:
NKJV.14: “This is My resting place forever; Here I will dwell, for I have desired it.
NKJV.15: I will abundantly bless her provision; I will satisfy her poor with bread.
NKJV.16: I will also clothe her priests with salvation,  And her saints shall shout aloud for joy.
NKJV.17: There I will make the horn of David grow;  I will prepare a lamp for My Anointed.
NKJV.18: His enemies I will clothe with shame, But upon Himself His crown shall flourish.”

ESV.1: Remember, O LORD, in David's favor, all the hardships he endured,
ESV.2: how he swore to the LORD and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob,
ESV.3: “I will not enter my house or get into my bed,
ESV.4: I will not give sleep to my eyes or slumber to my eyelids,
ESV.5: until I find a place for the LORD, a dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
ESV.6: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah; we found it in the fields of Jaar.
ESV.7: “Let us go to his dwelling place; let us worship at his footstool!”
ESV.8: Arise, O LORD, and go to your resting place, you and the ark of your might.
ESV.9: Let your priests be clothed with righteousness, and let your saints shout for joy.
ESV.10: For the sake of your servant David, do not turn away the face of your anointed one.
ESV.11: The LORD swore to David a sure oath from which he will not turn back: “One of the sons of your body  I will set on your throne.
ESV.12: If your sons keep my covenant and my testimonies that I shall teach them, their sons also forever shall sit on your throne.”
ESV.13: For the LORD has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his dwelling place:
ESV.14: “This is my resting place forever; here I will dwell, for I have desired it.
ESV.15: I will abundantly bless her provisions; I will satisfy her poor with bread.
ESV.16: Her priests I will clothe with salvation, and her saints will shout for joy.
ESV.17: There I will make a horn to sprout for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed.
ESV.18: His enemies I will clothe with shame, but on him his crown will shine.”

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents. Remember, O LORD, on David's behalf, All his affliction;
NASB.2: How he swore to the LORD And vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob,
NASB.3: 'Surely I will not enter my house, Nor lie on my bed;
NASB.4: I will not give sleep to my eyes Or slumber to my eyelids,
NASB.5: Until I find a place for the LORD, A dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob.'
NASB.6: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah, We found it in the field of Jaar.
NASB.7: Let us go into His dwelling place; Let us worship at His footstool.
NASB.8: Arise, O LORD, to Your resting place, You and the ark of Your strength.
NASB.9: Let Your priests be clothed with righteousness, And let Your godly ones sing for joy.
NASB.10: For the sake of David Your servant, Do not turn away the face of Your anointed.
NASB.11: The LORD has sworn to David A truth from which He will not turn back: 'Of the fruit of your body I will set upon your throne.
NASB.12: 'If your sons will keep My covenant And My testimony which I will teach them, Their sons also shall sit upon your throne forever.'
NASB.13: For the LORD has chosen Zion; He has desired it for His habitation.
NASB.14: 'This is My resting place forever; Here I will dwell, for I have desired it.
NASB.15: 'I will abundantly bless her provision; I will satisfy her needy with bread.
NASB.16: 'Her priests also I will clothe with salvation, And her godly ones will sing aloud for joy.
NASB.17: 'There I will cause the horn of David to spring forth; I have prepared a lamp for Mine anointed.
NASB.18: 'His enemies I will clothe with shame, But upon himself his crown shall shine.'

NLT.1:
LORD, remember David
and all that he suffered.
NLT.2:
He made a solemn promise to the LORD.
He vowed to the Mighty One of Israel,
NLT.3:
“I will not go home;
I will not let myself rest.
NLT.4:
I will not let my eyes sleep
nor close my eyelids in slumber
NLT.5:
until I find a place to build a house for the LORD,
a sanctuary for the Mighty One of Israel.”
NLT.6:
We heard that the Ark was in Ephrathah;
then we found it in the distant countryside of Jaar.
NLT.7:
Let us go to the sanctuary of the LORD;
let us worship at the footstool of his throne.
NLT.8:
Arise, O LORD, and enter your resting place,
along with the Ark, the symbol of your power.
NLT.9:
May your priests be clothed in godliness;
may your loyal servants sing for joy.
NLT.10:
For the sake of your servant David,
do not reject the king you have anointed.
NLT.11:
The LORD swore an oath to David
with a promise he will never take back:
“I will place one of your descendants
on your throne.
NLT.12:
If your descendants obey the terms of my covenant
and the laws that I teach them,
then your royal line
will continue forever and ever.”
NLT.13:
For the LORD has chosen Jerusalem ;
he has desired it for his home.
NLT.14:
“This is my resting place forever,” he said.
“I will live here, for this is the home I desired.
NLT.15:
I will bless this city and make it prosperous;
I will satisfy its poor with food.
NLT.16:
I will clothe its priests with godliness;
its faithful servants will sing for joy.
NLT.17:
Here I will increase the power of David;
my anointed one will be a light for my people.
NLT.18:
I will clothe his enemies with shame,
but he will be a glorious king.”

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. Yahweh, remember David and all his affliction,
WEB.2: how he swore to Yahweh, and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
WEB.3: “Surely I will not come into the structure of my house, nor go up into my bed;
WEB.4: I will not give sleep to my eyes, or slumber to my eyelids;
WEB.5: until I find out a place for Yahweh, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
WEB.6: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah. We found it in the field of Jaar:
WEB.7: “We will go into his dwelling place. We will worship at his footstool.
WEB.8: Arise, Yahweh, into your resting place; you, and the ark of your strength.
WEB.9: Let your priests be clothed with righteousness. Let your saints shout for joy!”
WEB.10: For your servant David’s sake, don’t turn away the face of your anointed one.
WEB.11: Yahweh has sworn to David in truth. He will not turn from it: “I will set the fruit of your body on your throne.
WEB.12: If your children will keep my covenant, my testimony that I will teach them, their children also will sit on your throne forever more.”
WEB.13: For Yahweh has chosen Zion. He has desired it for his habitation.
WEB.14: “This is my resting place forever. Here I will live, for I have desired it.
WEB.15: I will abundantly bless her provision. I will satisfy her poor with bread.
WEB.16: Her priests I will also clothe with salvation. Her saints will shout aloud for joy.
WEB.17: There I will make the horn of David to bud. I have ordained a lamp for my anointed.
WEB.18: I will clothe his enemies with shame, but on himself, his crown will be resplendent.”

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. O LORD, remember on David's behalf
All his hardship and affliction;
AMP.2:
How he swore to the LORD
And vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
AMP.3:
"I absolutely will not enter my house,
Nor get into my bed—
AMP.4:
I certainly will not permit my eyes to sleep
Nor my eyelids to slumber,
AMP.5:
Until I find a place for the LORD,
A dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob (Israel)."
AMP.6:
Behold, we heard of it at Ephrathah;
We found it in the field of Jaar.
AMP.7:
Let us go into His tabernacle;
Let us worship at His footstool.
AMP.8:
Arise, O LORD, to Your resting place,
You and the ark [the symbol] of Your strength.
AMP.9:
Let Your priests be clothed with righteousness (right living),
And let Your godly ones shout for joy.
AMP.10:
For the sake of Your servant David,
Do not turn away the face of Your anointed.
AMP.11:
The LORD swore to David
A truth from which He will not turn back:
"One of your descendants I will set upon your throne.
AMP.12:
"If your children will keep My covenant
And My testimony which I will teach them,
Their children also shall sit upon your throne forever."
AMP.13:
For the LORD has chosen Zion;
He has desired it for His dwelling place:
AMP.14:
"This is My resting place forever" [says the LORD];
"Here will I dwell, for I have desired it.
AMP.15:
"I will abundantly bless her provisions;
I will satisfy her poor with bread.
AMP.16:
"Her priests also I will clothe with salvation,
And her godly ones will shout aloud for joy.
AMP.17:
"There I will make the horn (strength) of David grow;
I have prepared a lamp for My anointed [fulfilling the promises].
AMP.18:
"His enemies I will clothe with shame,
But upon himself shall his crown shine."

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
O God, remember David,
remember all his troubles!
MSG.2:
And remember how he promised God,
made a vow to the Strong God of Jacob,
MSG.3:
"I'm not going home,
and I'm not going to bed,
MSG.4:
I'm not going to sleep,
not even take time to rest,
MSG.5:
Until I find a home for God,
a house for the Strong God of Jacob."
MSG.6:
Remember how we got the news in Ephrathah,
learned all about it at Jaar Meadows?
MSG.7:
We shouted, "Let's go to the shrine dedication!
Let's worship at God's own footstool!"
MSG.8:
Up, God, enjoy your new place of quiet repose,
you and your mighty covenant ark;
MSG.9:
Get your priests all dressed up in justice;
prompt your worshipers to sing this prayer:
MSG.10:
"Honor your servant David;
don't disdain your anointed one."
MSG.11:
God gave David his word,
he won't back out on this promise:
"One of your sons
I will set on your throne;
MSG.12:
If your sons stay true to my Covenant
and learn to live the way I teach them,
Their sons will continue the line—
always a son to sit on your throne.
MSG.13:
Yes—I, God, chose Zion,
the place I wanted for my shrine;
MSG.14:
This will always be my home;
this is what I want, and I'm here for good.
MSG.15:
I'll shower blessings on the pilgrims who come here,
and give supper to those who arrive hungry;
MSG.16:
I'll dress my priests in salvation clothes;
the holy people will sing their hearts out!
MSG.17:
Oh, I'll make the place radiant for David!
I'll fill it with light for my anointed!
MSG.18:
I'll dress his enemies in dirty rags,
but I'll make his crown sparkle with splendor."

NIV.1:
Psalm 132
A song of ascents.
O Lord, remember David
and all the hardships he endured.
NIV.2:
He swore an oath to the Lord
and made a vow to the Mighty One of Jacob:
NIV.3:
“I will not enter my house
or go to my bed—
NIV.4:
I will allow no sleep to my eyes,
no slumber to my eyelids,
NIV.5:
till I find a place for the Lord,
a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
NIV.6:
We heard it in Ephrathah,
we came upon it in the fields of Jaar:
NIV.7:
“Let us go to his dwelling place;
let us worship at his footstool—
NIV.8:
arise, O Lord, and come to your resting place,
you and the ark of your might.
NIV.9:
May your priests be clothed with righteousness;
may your saints sing for joy.”
NIV.10:
For the sake of David your servant,
do not reject your anointed one.
NIV.11:
The Lord swore an oath to David,
a sure oath that he will not revoke:
“One of your own descendants
I will place on your throne—
NIV.12:
if your sons keep my covenant
and the statutes I teach them,
then their sons will sit
on your throne for ever and ever.”
NIV.13:
For the Lord has chosen Zion,
he has desired it for his dwelling:
NIV.14:
“This is my resting place for ever and ever;
here I will sit enthroned, for I have desired it—
NIV.15:
I will bless her with abundant provisions;
her poor will I satisfy with food.
NIV.16:
I will clothe her priests with salvation,
and her saints will ever sing for joy.
NIV.17:
“Here I will make a horn grow for David
and set up a lamp for my anointed one.
NIV.18:
I will clothe his enemies with shame,
but the crown on his head will be resplendent.”

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, remember in David’s favor
all the hardships he endured;
NRSVCE.2:
how he swore to the LORD
and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob,
NRSVCE.3:
“I will not enter my house
or get into my bed;
NRSVCE.4:
I will not give sleep to my eyes
or slumber to my eyelids,
NRSVCE.5:
until I find a place for the LORD,
a dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
NRSVCE.6:
We heard of it in Ephrathah;
we found it in the fields of Jaar.
NRSVCE.7:
“Let us go to his dwelling place;
let us worship at his footstool.”
NRSVCE.8:
Rise up, O LORD, and go to your resting place,
you and the ark of your might.
NRSVCE.9:
Let your priests be clothed with righteousness,
and let your faithful shout for joy.
NRSVCE.10:
For your servant David’s sake
do not turn away the face of your anointed one.
NRSVCE.11:
The LORD swore to David a sure oath
from which he will not turn back:
“One of the sons of your body
I will set on your throne.
NRSVCE.12:
If your sons keep my covenant
and my decrees that I shall teach them,
their sons also, forevermore,
shall sit on your throne.”
NRSVCE.13:
For the LORD has chosen Zion;
he has desired it for his habitation:
NRSVCE.14:
“This is my resting place forever;
here I will reside, for I have desired it.
NRSVCE.15:
I will abundantly bless its provisions;
I will satisfy its poor with bread.
NRSVCE.16:
Its priests I will clothe with salvation,
and its faithful will shout for joy.
NRSVCE.17:
There I will cause a horn to sprout up for David;
I have prepared a lamp for my anointed one.
NRSVCE.18:
His enemies I will clothe with disgrace,
but on him, his crown will gleam.”

NET.1: O LORD, for David’s sake remember all his strenuous effort,
NET.2: and how he made a vow to the LORD, and swore an oath to the powerful ruler of Jacob.
NET.3: He said, “I will not enter my own home, or get into my bed.
NET.4: I will not allow my eyes to sleep, or my eyelids to slumber,
NET.5: until I find a place for the LORD, a fine dwelling place for the powerful ruler of Jacob.”
NET.6: Look, we heard about it in Ephrathah, we found it in the territory of Jaar.
NET.7: Let us go to his dwelling place! Let us worship before his footstool!
NET.8: Ascend, O LORD, to your resting place, you and the ark of your strength!
NET.9: May your priests be clothed with integrity! May your loyal followers shout for joy!
NET.10: For the sake of David, your servant, do not reject your chosen king!
NET.11: The LORD made a reliable promise to David; he will not go back on his word. He said, “I will place one of your descendants on your throne.
NET.12: If your sons keep my covenant and the rules I teach them, their sons will also sit on your throne forever.”
NET.13: Certainly the LORD has chosen Zion; he decided to make it his home.
NET.14: He said, “This will be my resting place forever; I will live here, for I have chosen it.
NET.15: I will abundantly supply what she needs; I will give her poor all the food they need.
NET.16: I will protect her priests, and her godly people will shout exuberantly.
NET.17: There I will make David strong; I have determined that my chosen king’s dynasty will continue.
NET.18: I will humiliate his enemies, and his crown will shine.
